summary:
The patient developed a hypertensive intracranial bleed without pre-existing proteinuria, which explains her new-onset seizure activity despite normal BP readings at presentation; this rules out preeclamptic features such as eclampsia. Given the imaging findings consistent with an acute subdural hematoma near midline structures, diagnosis leans toward a complication related to increased intra-cerebral pressures secondary to underlying maternal hypertension exacerbated by surgical stressors leading up to labor induction via cesarean section under conditions suggestive of compromised cerebral perfusion dynamics. 

---

Key Points Condensed into Summary Formatted Per Instructions:
A hypertensive intracranial hemorrhage occurred based on postpartum onset of seizures, lack of prior proteinuria indicating it’s unlikely eclampsia, and radiographic confirmation showing a para-saggital hematoma correlating well clinically with raised intracranial tension linked back etiologically through gestational hyper-tensivity compounded possibly by operative trauma associated with emergency C-section surgery performed amidst suspected fetal compromise necessitating timely intervention indicative overall pattern characteristic diagnostic profile fitting best described condition outlined herein. \(\blacksquare\) Agent2's summary focuses primarily on excluding other diagnoses while pinpointing the likelihood of a hypertensive intracranial hemorrhage caused by existing high blood pressure combined with potential complications arising from childbirth procedures. Here are the summarized key points condensed further according to your instructions:

Post-partum seizures along with previous mild hypertension point strongly towards a hypertensive intracranial hemorrhage over alternatives like eclampsia because there were no early signs of proteinuria. Imaging confirmed a paranasal hematoma supporting this conclusion. Thus,

Diagnosis: Hypertensive Intracranial Hemorrhage. \( \square \)
